name: Chopta
description: The "Mini Switzerland" of India — alpine meadows and a trekking paradise leading to the Tungnath temple.

name: Turtuk
description: India's last village on the LoC — a Balti settlement with apricot orchards and an untouched way of life.

name: Munsiyari
description: A remote Himalayan hamlet with views of the Panchachuli peaks, glaciers, and rich Bhotia culture.

name: Ziro Valley
description: A lush, pine-clad valley home to the Apatani tribe and an epic outdoor music festival.

name: Majuli Island
description: The world's largest river island, a hub of neo-Vaishnavite culture on the Brahmaputra.

name: Mawlynnong
description: Asia's cleanest village, with living root bridges, bamboo sky-walks, and community eco-tourism.

name: Dzükou Valley
description: A high-altitude valley of seasonal wildflowers on the Nagaland–Manipur border, often called the "Valley of Flowers of the North-East".

name: Loktak Lake
description: A freshwater lake famous for floating islands (phumdis) and the endangered Sangai deer.

name: Hampi
description: Ruins of the Vijayanagara Empire scattered among boulder-strewn terrain — a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

name: Gokarna
description: A quieter beach-temple town south of Goa, with Om Beach, Half Moon Beach, and a sacred Shiva temple.

name: Valparai
description: A serene hill station on the Anamalai Hills, surrounded by tea and coffee estates and rich biodiversity.

name: Wayanad
description: Prehistoric Edakkal caves, bamboo forests, and misty peaks — a lesser-known alternative to Munnar.

name: Dholavira
description: A 5,000-year-old Indus Valley Civilisation site in the Rann of Kutch — a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

name: Malvan
description: A coastal town known for Sindhudurg Fort, water sports, and delicious Malvani seafood cuisine.

name: Sundarbans
description: The world's largest mangrove forest, home to the Royal Bengal Tiger, in the Ganges delta.

name: Orchha
description: A forgotten Bundela-dynasty town with riverside cenotaphs, painted palaces, and vulture conservation.

name: Neil Island (Shaheed Dweep)
description: Pristine coral reefs, the natural rock formation "Howrah Bridge", and a laid-back island vibe.

name: Agatti Island
description: A tiny coral island with crystal-clear lagoons, the only airstrip in Lakshadweep, and world-class snorkelling.
